assumptions.model: tok_s' = tok_s / ((1-opt_share)/s_c + opt_share/s_m); compile speeds model only, fused-muon speeds optimizer only

finding: Compile alone is INSUFFICIENT: for c03 (opt_share 0.41) compile-alone needs s_c>=2.37x (implausible). §3 clears only with BOTH compile AND fused-muon, each ~1.5x — but 1.5/1.5 lands at 25251, 0.8% SHORT of 25463 (thin margin; one lever must slightly over-deliver, e.g. fused 1.53x at compile 1.5x). #329 (fused-muon) is therefore LOAD-BEARING on the critical path, not a side optimization.
decision_rule: Break BOTH walls: #373 (compile) + #329 (fused-muon). Re-bench c03 SOLO compiled+fused. Clean-re-bench the 4 contaminated candidates (parallel dispatch made their tok_s lower-bounds only). Escalation trigger: ONLY if a real compiled+fused solo re-bench is still < 25463 tok/s does the <=1-day bar become the user's call (wall-day fraction). Budget cut is NOT ours — only the user reduces scope.
caveats: s_c/s_m independence + multiplicativity is an approximation; binding verdict = measured re-bench
4 of 5 candidates contaminated by 4-way parallel GPU dispatch (tok_s = lower bounds)
c03 solo is the only valid §3 reference in this batch
